2603.23178.title: SAiW: Source-Attributable Invisible Watermarking for Proactive Deepfake Defense
2603.23178.paper_summary: Deepfakes generated by modern generative models pose a serious threat to information integrity, digital identity, and public trust. Existing detection methods are largely reactive, attempting to identify manipulations after they occur and often failing to generalize across evolving generation techniques. This motivates the need for proactive mechanisms that secure media authenticity at the time of creation. In this work, we introduce SAiW, a Source-Attributed Invisible watermarking Framework for proactive deepfake defense and media provenance verification. Unlike conventional watermarking methods that treat watermark payloads as generic signals, SAiW formulates watermark embedding as a source-conditioned representation learning problem, where watermark identity encodes the originating source and modulates the embedding process to produce discriminative and traceable signatures. The framework integrates feature-wise linear modulation to inject source identity into the embedding network, enabling scalable multi-source watermark generation. A perceptual guidance module derived from human visual system priors ensures that watermark perturbations remain visually imperceptible while maintaining robustness. In addition, a dual-purpose forensic decoder simultaneously reconstructs the embedded watermark and performs source attribution, providing both automated verification and interpretable forensic evidence. Extensive experiments across multiple deepfake datasets demonstrate that SAiW achieves high perceptual quality while maintaining strong robustness against compression, filtering, noise, geometric transformations, and adversarial perturbations. By binding digital media to its origin through invisible yet verifiable markers, SAiW enables reliable authentication and source attribution, providing a scalable foundation for proactive deepfake defense and trustworthy media provenance.
2603.23178.paper_summary_zh: 現代生成模型產生的深偽影像對資訊完整性、數位身份和公眾信任構成了嚴重威脅。現有的檢測方法主要是反應性的，試圖在操控發生後識別它們，並且常常無法在不斷演變的生成技術中進行泛化。這促使了對於在創建時保障媒體真實性的主動機制的需求。在這項工作中，我們介紹了SAiW，一種源屬性隱形水印框架，用於主動深偽防禦和媒體來源驗證。與將水印有效載荷視為通用信號的傳統水印方法不同，SAiW將水印嵌入公式化為一個源條件的表示學習問題，其中水印身份編碼了來源，並調節嵌入過程以產生可區分且可追溯的簽名。該框架整合了特徵級線性調製，將來源身份注入嵌入網絡，使得可擴展的多來源水印生成成為可能。一個源自人類視覺系統先驗的感知引導模塊確保水印擾動在視覺上保持不可察覺，同時保持穩健性。此外，一個雙用途的法醫解碼器同時重建嵌入的水印並執行來源歸屬，提供自動驗證和可解釋的法醫證據。對多個深偽數據集的廣泛實驗表明，SAiW在保持強穩健性對抗壓縮、過濾、噪聲、幾何變換和對抗擾動的同時，實現了高感知質量。通過將數位媒體與其來源綁定，透過不可見但可驗證的標記，SAiW實現了可靠的身份驗證和來源歸屬，為主動深偽防禦和可信媒體來源提供了一個可擴展的基礎。
2603.23178.author: Bibek Das et.al.
2603.23178.authors: Bibek Das, Chandranath Adak, Soumi Chattopadhyay, Zahid Akhtar, Soumya Dutta
